topic.what: AGA (American Gastroenterological Association) expert review on the diagnosis and management of atrophic gastritis, covering serological screening (pepsinogen I/II ratio, gastrin-17), histopathological staging (OLGA/OLGIM), and surveillance strategies.
topic.how: Expert consensus review synthesizing evidence on non-invasive biomarkers for atrophy detection, histopathological staging systems, and risk-stratified surveillance intervals for gastric cancer prevention.
topic.why_matters: Atrophic gastritis is the key precancerous condition in the stomach; standardized diagnosis and management guidance from a major US gastroenterology society directly shapes clinical practice for early detection and cancer prevention.

characteristics.what_it_measures: Integration of serological, endoscopic, and histopathological approaches to atrophic gastritis diagnosis
characteristics.value: comprehensive multi-modal diagnostic and surveillance framework
characteristics.vs_baseline: First major AGA expert review dedicated to atrophic gastritis, filling a gap in US-focused guidance
characteristics.evidence: Expert consensus based on systematic evidence review
characteristics.context: US clinical practice context; may differ from European (MAPS II) or Asian guidelines

good_for: Diagnosing and staging atrophic gastritis using serological and histopathological methods
Implementing pepsinogen-based screening programs for gastric atrophy
Risk-stratifying patients for endoscopic surveillance using OLGA/OLGIM
not_for: Populations where pepsinogen assay cutoffs have not been locally validated
requires: Access to pepsinogen I/II and gastrin-17 serological assays

novelty: First dedicated AGA expert review consolidating atrophic gastritis management for US practice
Integrates serological screening (pepsinogen, gastrin-17) with histopathological staging into a single clinical pathway
open_problems: Optimal pepsinogen cutoff values across different ethnic populations
Head-to-head comparison of serological versus endoscopic screening strategies
Long-term prospective validation of OLGA/OLGIM-based surveillance intervals
Role of AI-assisted endoscopy in atrophic gastritis detection
